title: I Taught Myself To Live Simply
content:
I taught myself to live simply and wisely,
to look at the sky and pray to God,
and to wander long before evening
to tire my superfluous worries.

title: Lying In Me
content:
Lying in me, as though it were a white
Stone in the depths of a well, is one
Memory that I cannot, will not, fight:
It is happiness, and it is pain

title: White Night
content:

Nor lit the candles,
You don't know, don't care,
That tired I haven't the strength

title: I Wrung My Hands
content:

"Why are you pale, what makes you reckless?"
-- Because I have made my loved one drunk

title: Requiem
content:
Not under foreign skies
Nor under foreign wings protected -
I shared all this with my own people
There, where misfortune had abandoned us.
[1961]

title: Sunbeam
content:

It is pale, thin, straight.
Since morning I have been silent,

content:

title: Departure
content:
Although this land is not my own,
I will remember its inland sea
and the waters that are so cold
the sand as white
as old bones, the pine trees

title: Why Is This Age Worse...?
content:
Why is this age worse than earlier ages?
In a stupor of grief and dread
have we not fingered the foulest wounds
and left them unhealed by our hands

title: I Hear The Oriole's Always-Grieving Voice
content:

In the sickle's serpentine hiss

title: I Don't Know If You're Alive Or Dead
content:
I don't know if you're alive or dead.
Can you on earth be sought,
Or only when the sunsets fade
Be mourned serenely in my thought?
